statement.en: Randomised Field Trial of Process-Control System Diagnostics for Yield Optimisation in Ghanaian Industrial Operations
statement.summary: Industrial process-control systems in many developing economies often operate sub-optimally, with limited empirical evidence on the efficacy of systematic diagnostic interventions for yield improvement. This study aimed to quantify the causal impact of a structured process-control diagnostic protocol on operational yield within Ghanaian industrial settings. A randomised field trial was conducted across multiple manufacturing sites. Treatment plants received the diagnostic intervention, while control plants continued standard operations. Yield was measured as the mass ratio of final product to raw material input. The treatment effect was estimated using a linear mixed model: $Y_{ij} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 T_{ij} + \mu_j + \epsilon_{ij}$, where $Y_{ij}$ is yield for unit $i$ in firm $j$, $T_{ij

proposer: Kwame Asante, Kofi Mensah Ankrah, Ama Serwaa Boateng

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint (dated 2026-04-25, future-dated or recent) reporting empirical trial results. The abstract presents a structured RCT design with causal inference methodology, but the document is incomplete (abstract cuts off mid-equation). As a field trial not yet peer-reviewed or validated in formal publication, it occupies provisional epistemic status. The intervention itself (diagnostic protocol) is implementable and the measurement (yield ratio) is operationalizable, but the causal claims remain unvalidated until results are published and reproduced.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 診断という光が暗い工場の不効率を照らし、その露出度合いが利益の増分を決める。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 二つの工場の間に立つ診断という矢印が、上昇する効率と下降する廃棄物の交差を示す。
